strengths: Freehold title with no encumbrance — clean acquisition with minimal legal risk
0.8 km walk to Permas Jaya LRT station supports Transit-Oriented Development (TOD) positioning and premium pricing
Commercial C2 zoning with 3.5x plot ratio unlocks high-density mixed-use development
Strong blended PSF of RM 680 supported by proximity to Singapore demand corridor and CIQ connectivity
weaknesses: Land cost at RM 45M (RM 186 psf) is at the upper end for Permas Jaya submarket — leaves limited buffer on downside
Projected NDP margin of 19.0% is below the 20% internal benchmark, contingent on full absorption at RM 680 psf
30% Bumi quota requirement will constrain open market pricing on a significant portion of units
opportunities: RTS Link completion (2026) will materially boost cross-border demand from Singapore-based buyers and investors
Retail podium (18,000 sqft) can attract F&B or convenience anchor tenant to differentiate from competing residential projects
Johor-Singapore SEZ designation may drive institutional and corporate demand for SOHO and serviced apartment inventory
threats: Johor Bahru residential market remains oversupplied — absorption assumptions of 70%/yr may be optimistic given current unsold stock levels
Construction cost inflation risk: RM 220 psf hard cost assumption is tight given material price pressures post-2024
Competing TOD projects (e.g. Bukit Chagar, Medini) within 10 km targeting the same Singapore-linked buyer pool

recommendation.headline: Attractive TOD land with sound fundamentals, but margin headroom is thin — proceed subject to tighter cost controls and pre-sales commitments.
recommendation.rationale: The Permas Jaya parcel benefits from a rare freehold TOD position with direct LRT access, well-suited for BRDB's mixed-use capabilities. However, the projected NDP margin of 19.0% on NDV sits below the 20% internal threshold, and the RM 680 psf blended ASP assumption carries execution risk in a currently subdued JB market. The deal is viable but requires disciplined cost management and early pre-sales validation before committing to SPA execution.
keyConditions: Secure a minimum 30% pre-sales commitment before construction commencement to de-risk absorption assumptions
Renegotiate land cost to RM 42M or below, or structure a deferred payment mechanism to improve IRR to ≥18%
Appoint a quantity surveyor to validate the RM 220 psf build cost assumption before Board Approval on 15 Apr 2026
